title: Out-of-fold categorical hazard encodings
summary: Regularized target encoding is strong for high-cardinality categorical predictors, and scikit-learn documents cross-fitting as important to avoid overfitting.
rationale: Regularized target encoding is strong for high-cardinality categorical predictors, and scikit-learn documents cross-fitting as important to avoid overfitting. Current runs rely mainly on raw categorical columns and hand-built categorical crosses, not explicit OOF stop-rate priors.
implementation_hint: Within CV folds, compute smoothed OOF mean `PitNextLap` for keys such as `Race+LapNumber`, `Race+Compound+TyreLifeBin`, `Driver+Stint+TyreLifeBin`, and `Year+Race+Stint`. For test, fit encodings on all train rows with shrinkage to global mean. Feed these numeric priors alongside raw categories.
expected_effect: Can capture empirical pit windows and driver/race tendencies while staying target-safe inside validation.
risk: Random row CV may still leak temporal/race information through fold mixing; use grouped or chronological folds for the encoder test if possible.
